preprocessing.map_weight_function: Maps vehicle_weight (numeric 0.2-1.0 in this pipeline) to est_vehicle_width via substring checks for 'heavy'/'medium'/'two' -- NOTE: on the current numeric vehicle_weight column this always falls through to the 1.8 default, so est_vehicle_width is effectively constant for all rows in the current dataset.
preprocessing.missing_value_strategy: median fill, per-column, from training set

target_definition: Binary target_class derived from impact_label (Stage 3 unsupervised Isolation Forest score, built from road_vulnerability_score, vehicle_weight, junction_degree): 1 = top 20% (Bottleneck), 0 = bottom 20% (Harmless), middle 60% excluded from training.
known_caveats: road_vulnerability_score and junction_degree are DIRECT inputs to the impact_label target this model was trained to predict -- validation AUC is inflated by this overlap, not purely a discovered relationship between violation patterns and severity.
est_vehicle_width is constant (1.8) for all rows on the current numeric vehicle_weight data -- contributes no real signal.
frc_vulnerability is a hardcoded lookup table, not measured from live TomTom/OSM data, despite earlier code comments.
Train/test split was a random stratified split, not grouped by cluster_id -- some leakage across nearby/same-location violations between train and validation is possible.
